Package org.apache.juneau.commons.logging

Class Logger

java.lang.Object
java.util.logging.Logger
org.apache.juneau.commons.logging.Logger
public class Logger extends Logger
Extended logger that provides convenience methods for logging formatted messages.

This class extends Logger and uses delegation internally to wrap an underlying Logger instance. This design provides:

  • Type compatibility - extends Logger for use anywhere a Logger is expected
  • Flexibility - can wrap existing logger instances
  • Convenience methods for formatted logging using Shorts.fs(String, Object...)
Features:
  • All standard Logger functionality via delegation
  • Convenience methods for formatted logging at all log levels
  • Lazy evaluation of formatted messages using Shorts.fs(String, Object...)
  • Support for both MessageFormat-style and printf-style formatting
Format Support:

The formatting methods support both MessageFormat-style and printf-style formatting:

  • Printf-style: "%s", "%d", "%.2f", etc.
  • MessageFormat-style: "{0}", "{1,number}", etc.
  • Un-numbered MessageFormat: "{}" - Sequential placeholders
Example:

Logger logger = Logger.getLogger(MyClass.class.getName()); // Formatted logging at different levels logger.severe("Error processing user {0}: {1}", userId, error); logger.warning("Failed to connect to %s after %d attempts", hostname, attempts); logger.info("Processing {} items", count); logger.fine("Debug value: %s", debugValue);

See Also:
  • Shorts.fs(String, Object...)
  • StringUtils.format(String, Object...)
  • Logger

  • Constructor Details

    • Logger

      protected Logger(Logger delegate)
      Protected constructor - wraps an existing logger instance.
      Parameters:
      delegate - The underlying logger to delegate to.

  • Method Details

    • getLogger

      public static Logger getLogger(String name)
      Creates a logger for the specified name.

      This method returns the same Logger instance for a given name, ensuring that listeners attached to a logger persist across multiple calls to getLogger(String). Logger instances are cached and automatically created using the underlying Logger.getLogger(String).

      Parameters:
      name - The logger name.
      Returns:
      A logger instance (cached and reused for the same name).

    • getLogger

      public static Logger getLogger(Class<?> clazz)
      Creates a logger for the specified class.
      Parameters:
      clazz - The class.
      Returns:
      A logger instance.

    • captureEvents

      public LogRecordCapture captureEvents()
      Creates a log record capture for testing.

      This method creates a LogRecordCapture that will capture all log records from this logger. The capture should be closed when done (typically using try-with-resources).

      Returns:
      A LogRecordCapture instance.
